meta_title: For Students: client workflow to Solve can't finish projects (Intermediate Tutorial)
meta_description: Learn how to client workflow and overcome can't finish projects. A Intermediate-level Tutorial using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Stop Abandoning Projects: The Student's Client Workflow Guide
content.seo_description: Stop leaving side projects unfinished. Learn a professional client-focused workflow using Antigravity and Claude Code to ship your portfolio apps faster.
content.answer_block: The root cause of unfinished projects is a lack of defined scope and feedback loops. By adopting a 'client workflow'—treating your personal project like a high-stakes delivery—you force accountability. Use Antigravity to structure requirements and Claude Code to execute features, ensuring you ship functional, production-ready code every time.
content.intro:
You stare at a graveyard of half-finished GitHub repositories. The initial spark of excitement fades, replaced by the crushing weight of scope creep and technical debt. You aren't failing because you lack talent; you are failing because you lack a system. Every professional engineer understands that code is merely the output; the workflow is the engine that drives the delivery.

Today, we stop the cycle of abandonment. We are replacing your chaotic coding sessions with a rigorous, client-ready workflow. By simulating a professional environment using Antigravity for architecture and Claude Code for implementation, you will transform your scattered ideas into high-value portfolio pieces that prove you can ship, not just experiment.

steps.title: Define the MVP Scope
steps.description: Establish a strict 48-hour feature set. If it isn't core to the app's value, it's cut.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a CTO. Review my app idea and define a strict MVP scope that can be built in 48 hours. Focus on one core user journey.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Create a project specification file (spec.md) based on the core MVP features identified.'

steps.title: Architect the Data Flow
steps.description: Map the database schema and API endpoints before writing a single line of application code.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Design a relational schema for my MVP. Provide the SQL or Prisma schema and explain the data flow between the user and the backend.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Initialize the database schema based on the spec.md architecture.'

steps.title: Rapid Interface Prototyping
steps.description: Build the UI using component-driven development to ensure visual consistency.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate the component hierarchy for the MVP interface. Focus on reusable UI blocks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create the core UI components in /src/components based on the generated hierarchy.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to implement one feature at a time, testing as you go.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Break down the authentication feature into 3 small, testable tasks.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement the first authentication task: user registration endpoint with validation.'

steps.title: Deployment and Feedback Loop
steps.description: Deploy early to a staging environment to treat your app like a live client project.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a checklist for production readiness, focusing on security and environment variables.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Generate a deployment script for Vercel and verify all environment variables are set.'

pro_tips: Never build UI logic until the API contract is signed off in your spec.md file.
Use Claude Code to write your integration tests before you write the actual business logic.
Treat your commit messages as 'client status updates' to practice professional communication.
If a feature takes more than 4 hours to implement, break it into two smaller sub-tasks immediately.
Always deploy to a live URL on day one; seeing a real link prevents you from abandoning the project.
content.next_steps: Once your MVP is live, share the URL in our community discord. Use the feedback to iterate on your next version, treating your users as the ultimate stakeholder.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting your ideas die in a private repo. Master the workflow that separates hobbyists from professional software engineers. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
